docsTags.name: virtualProp

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
header - Content is placed at the top and is used to
expand or collapse the accordion item.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text:
content - Content is placed below the header and is
shown or hidden based on expanded state.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: header - The wrapper element for the header slot.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: content - The wrapper element for the content slot.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text:
expanded - The expanded element. Can be used in combination
with the `header` and `content` parts (i.e. `::part(header expanded)`).

props.name: delay
props.docs: Property specifying the delay before the animation starts in milliseconds.

props.name: direction
props.docs: Animation direction property

props.name: duration
props.docs: A property indicating the duration of the animation in milliseconds.

props.name: easing
props.docs: Property specifying the release function for the animation.

props.name: endDelay
props.docs: A property specifying the delay after the animation has finished, in milliseconds.

props.name: fill
props.docs: Property defining the animation fill mode (before and after animation).

props.name: name
props.docs:
A property defining the name of the animation to be used. For a list of all available animations, see 
[`animate.css`](https://animate.style).

docsTags.name: slot

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: native - The native HTML button or anchor element that wraps all child elements.

props.name: active
props.docs: If `true`, a `check` icon will appear in the button

props.name: color
props.docs:
The color to be used from the application color palette. The default options are `"primary"`, `"warning"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"warning"`, and `"neutral"`.
For more information on colors, visit [Customization Basics](/theming/basics).

props.name: dialog
props.docs: Associates a button with a specific event. When the button is clicked, the named event is triggered, allowing you to implement actions such as opening a dialog box or executing a custom event. See the dialog component for more information.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the button.

props.name: fill
props.docs: Set to "link" for a transparent button without borders, to "outline" for a transparent button with border, or to "solid" with filled background. The default fill is "solid".

props.name: size
props.docs: Set to "small" for a button with a smaller height and padding, or to "large" for a button with a larger height and padding. By default, the size is set to the default.

props.name: toggle
props.docs: Allows you to assign two different states to a button and toggle between them by clicking. Setting a value assigns a default state to the button. The child element must contain a slot with the value `toggle` and also a `state` property with one of the two available state values.

events.docs: Called when the button is clicked

Specifies the background color of the checkbox. The default options are `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information, go to: [theming](/theming/basics).

props.docs: The number of columns by which the column should be shifted.

props.docs: The number of columns by which the column is to be shifted on screens of size `lg`.

props.name: offsetMd

props.name: offsetXs

props.name: order
props.docs: Sets the column order in the row by adding the values 1 to 5, `"first"` and `"last"`

props.name: size
props.docs: Sets the column size in terms of how many columns it should occupy out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: sizeLg
props.docs: Sets the column size on `lg` size screens in terms of how many columns it should occupy out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: sizeMd
props.docs: Sets the column size on `md` size screens in terms of how many columns to take up out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: sizeSm
props.docs: Sets the column size on `sm` size screens in terms of how many columns to take up out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: sizeXl
props.docs: Sets the column size on `xl` sized screens in terms of how many columns it should occupy out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: sizeXs
props.docs: Sets the column size on `xs` sized screens in terms of how many columns it should occupy out of the total number of available columns. If `"auto"` is specified, the column will be the size of its contents.

props.name: wrap

For more information, go to: [theming](/theming/basics).

props.docs: It serves as an alternative text to describe the content of the image, which will be displayed if the image is not loaded. It is also used by screen readers.

props.name: src
props.docs: Specifies the source URL of the image file to be displayed on the web page.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: start - The content is placed to the left of the input in the LTR and to the right in the RTL in the form of a new section.

docsTags.name: slot
docsTags.text: end - The content is displayed to the right of the input in the LTR and to the left in the RTL as a new section.

props.name: clearable
props.docs: If `true`, it will allow the contents of the input to be cleared using the button.

props.name: custom-error-display
props.docs: Activates the option to display a custom error message. The `message` property is used to set it.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, disables the ability to interact with the input

props.name: label
props.docs: Defines the descriptive text of the input.

props.name: max
props.docs: Defines the maximum numeric value in the input (must not be less than its minimum value - the `min` attribute).

props.name: maxlength
props.docs: Specifies the maximum number of characters the user must enter (Valid for inputs of type `text`, `email`, `search`, `password`, `tel`, or `url`).

props.name: min
props.docs: Defines the minimum numeric value in the input (must not be less than its minimum value - the `min` attribute).

props.name: minlength
props.docs: Specifies the minimum number of characters the user must enter (Valid for inputs of type `text`, `email`, `search`, `password`, `tel` or `url`).

props.name: message
props.docs: Defines the text of the custom error message.

props.name: pattern
props.docs: Regex by which the value is checked. The pattern must correspond to the whole value, not just part of it. Use the title attribute to describe the pattern to help the user. This attribute is used if the attribute value is of type `"text"`, `"search"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`, `"email"`, `"date"` or `"password"`, otherwise it is ignored. If the attribute type is `"date"`, `pattern` will only be used in browsers that do not support the `"date"` input type natively. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/date for more information.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs: Defines the input placeholder text.

props.name: readonly
props.docs: If `true`, disables the ability to write to the input

props.name: required
props.docs: If `true`, sets the input as mandatory for completion.

props.name: type
props.docs: Sets the input type.

props.name: validate-on-change
props.docs: If `true`, sets the validation of the input content after each change is made.

props.name: value
props.docs: Input value.

events.docs: Called when the `Clear` button is clicked.
The `wj-input:input` event is fired whenever the user changes the input value. This usually happens every time a key is pressed while the user is typing.

props.name: date
props.docs: Specifies a date or timestamp in ISO format or Unix timestamp format (in seconds). If no date attribute is specified, the component defaults to the current date and time.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, elements cannot be arranged using drag and drop functionality.

props.name: reverse
props.docs: If `true`, the elements will be arranged in reverse order.

slots.name: handle
slots.docs: The content is placed at the beginning of the element.

props.name: root
props.docs:
The root path to use when matching URLs. By default, this is set to "/", but you can specify
an alternate prefix for all URL paths.

props.name: useHash
props.docs:
The router can work in two "modes":
- With hash: `/index.html#/path/to/page`
- Without hash: `/path/to/page`

Using one or another might depend in the requirements of your app and/or where it's deployed.

Usually "hash-less" navigation works better for SEO and it's more user friendly too, but it might
requires additional server-side configuration in order to work properly.

On the other side hash-navigation is much easier to deploy, it even works over the file protocol.

By default, this property is `true`, change to `false` to allow hash-less URLs.

props.name: counter
props.docs: If `true`, adds a counter below the element showing the number of characters inserted and also the maximum number allowed.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the textarea will not be writable.

props.name: label
props.docs: Defines the text description for the textarea element.

props.name: maxlength
props.docs: Sets the maximum number of characters allowed. Used in conjunction with the `counter` attribute.

props.name: name
props.docs: Sets the name attribute of the inner textarea of the element. This is important, for example, when used in forms.

props.name: resize
props.docs: Defines the behavior of element resizing. If it is set to `"auto"`, the element size is automatically adjusted to the content.

props.name: rows
props.docs: Defines the number of visible lines of text in the element.

props.name: spellcheck
props.docs: If `true`, the spell checker will be activated in textarea.

props.name: Option
props.docs: Setting it to `"standard"` will display a textarea in the style of the standard HTML textarea element.

events.docs: Fired when an element loses focus.
It is invoked after a change in textarea.
Called when the element gets focus.

The `ionInput` event is fired each time the user modifies the textarea's value.
Unlike the `ionChange` event, the `ionInput` event is fired for each alteration
to the textarea's value. This typically happens for each keystroke as the user types.

When `clearOnEdit` is enabled, the `ionInput` event will be fired when
the user clears the textarea by performing a keydown event.

docsTags.text: button - Any button element that is displayed inside of the toast.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: button cancel - Any button element with role "cancel" that is displayed inside of the toast.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: container - The element that wraps all child elements.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: header - The header text of the toast.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: message - The body text of the toast.

docsTags.name: part
docsTags.text: icon - The icon that appears next to the toast content.

props.name: animated
props.docs: If `true`, the toast will animate.

props.name: buttons
props.docs: An array of buttons for the toast.

props.name: color
props.docs:
The color to use from your application's color palette.
Default options are: `"primary"`, `"secondary"`, `"tertiary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"light"`, `"medium"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, see [theming](/theming/basics).

props.name: cssClass
props.docs:
Additional classes to apply for custom CSS. If multiple classes are
provided they should be separated by spaces.

props.name: duration
props.docs:
How many milliseconds to wait before hiding the toast. By default, it will show
until `dismiss()` is called.

props.name: enterAnimation
props.docs: Animation to use when the toast is presented.

props.name: header
props.docs: Header to be shown in the toast.

props.name: htmlAttributes
props.docs: Additional attributes to pass to the toast.

props.name: icon
props.docs:
The name of the icon to display, or the path to a valid SVG file. See `ion-icon`.
https://ionic.io/ionicons

props.name: isOpen
props.docs:
If `true`, the toast will open. If `false`, the toast will close.
Use this if you need finer grained control over presentation, otherwise
just use the toastController or the `trigger` property.
Note: `isOpen` will not automatically be set back to `false` when
the toast dismisses. You will need to do that in your code.

props.name: keyboardClose
props.docs: If `true`, the keyboard will be automatically dismissed when the overlay is presented.

props.name: layout
props.docs:
Defines how the message and buttons are laid out in the toast.
'baseline': The message and the buttons will appear on the same line.
Message text may wrap within the message container.
'stacked': The buttons containers and message will stack on top
of each other. Use this if you have long text in your buttons.

props.name: LeaveAnimation
props.docs: Animation to use when the toast is dismissed.

props.name: message
props.docs:
Message to be shown in the toast.
This property accepts custom HTML as a string.
Content is parsed as plaintext by default.
`innerHTMLTemplatesEnabled` must be set to `true` in the Ionic config
before custom HTML can be used.

props.name: fashion
props.docs: The mode determines which platform styles to use.

props.name: Position
props.docs:
The starting position of the toast on the screen. Can be tweaked further
using the `positionAnchor` property.

props.name: positionAnchor
props.docs:
The element to anchor the toast's position to. Can be set as a direct reference
or the ID of the element. With `position="bottom"`, the toast will sit above the
chosen element. With `position="top"`, the toast will sit below the chosen element.
With `position="middle"`, the value of `positionAnchor` is ignored.

props.name: swipeGesture
props.docs:
If set to 'vertical', the Toast can be dismissed with
a swipe gesture. The swipe direction is determined by
the value of the `position` property:
`top`: The Toast can be swiped up to dismiss.
`bottom`: The Toast can be swiped down to dismiss.
`middle`: The Toast can be swiped up or down to dismiss.

props.name: translucent
props.docs:
If `true`, the toast will be translucent.
Only applies when the mode is `"ios"` and the device supports
[`backdrop-filter`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/backdrop-filter#Browser_compatibility).

props.name: trigger
props.docs:
An ID corresponding to the trigger element that
causes the toast to open when clicked.

methods.name: dismiss

parameters.name: data
parameters.docs: Any data to emit in the dismiss events.

parameters.name: role
parameters.docs:
The role of the element that is dismissing the toast.
This can be useful in a button handler for determining which button was
clicked to dismiss the toast.
Some examples include: `"cancel"`, `"destructive"`, "selected"`, and `"backdrop"`.

parameters.name: data
parameters.docs: Any data to emit in the dismiss events.

parameters.name: role
parameters.docs:
The role of the element that is dismissing the toast.
This can be useful in a button handler for determining which button was
clicked to dismiss the toast.
Some examples include: `"cancel"`, `"destructive"`, "selected"`, and `"backdrop"`.

methods.docs: Dismiss the toast overlay after it has been presented.

role The role of the element that is dismissing the toast.
This can be useful in a button handler for determining which button was
clicked to dismiss the toast.
Some examples include: `"cancel"`, `"destructive"`, "selected"`, and `"backdrop"`.

methods.name: onDidDismiss
methods.docs: Returns a promise that resolves when the toast did dismiss.

methods.name: onWillDismiss

props.name: color
props.docs:
The color to use from your application's color palette. The default options are `"primary"`, `"success"`, `"warning"`, `"danger"`, `"complete"`, and `"dark"`.
For more information on colors, visit [Customization Basics](/theming/basics).

src/components/datetime/datetime-interfa.declaration: export type DatetimeHighlight = { date: string } & DatetimeHighlightStyle;

src/components/input/input-interface.ts:.docstring:
Values are converted to strings when emitted which is
why we do not have a `number` type here even though the
`value` prop accepts a `number` type.
